pattern: Simulating each rotation greedily (board as many waiting customers as the gondola allows) and tracking the best profit seen so far turns a search over when-to-stop into a single linear pass.
hints: Simulate rotation by rotation: before each rotation, add any newly arriving customers to a waiting pool, then board up to 4 of them into the gondola about to leave.
Every rotation costs the same fixed running cost and earns boardingCost per customer boarded that rotation, so profit after each rotation can be tracked as a running total.

Keep rotating until there are no customers left waiting and none left to arrive, recording the rotation count at which the running profit was highest; if that best profit never exceeds 0, the answer is -1.

edge_inputs.description: profitable from the start, stopping right when customers run out
waiting customers spill over past the arrivals list
a single arrival wave smaller than gondola capacity
running the wheel is never worth it, profit never turns positive
profit peaks early then declines as running cost outweighs a thin trickle of customers

stress.description: long arrival sequence with random costs
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 100000))
    customers = [rng.randint(0, 50) for _ in range(n)]
    boarding = rng.randint(1, 100)
    running = rng.randint(1, 100)
    return [customers, boarding, running]

oracle_python:
class Solution:
    def minOperationsMaxProfit(self, customers, boardingCost, runningCost):
        waiting = 0
        profits = []
        profit = 0
        n = len(customers)
        i = 0
        while i < n or waiting > 0:
            if i < n:
                waiting += customers[i]
                i += 1
            board = waiting if waiting < 4 else 4
            waiting -= board
            profit += board * boardingCost - runningCost
            profits.append(profit)
        best = -1
        best_idx = -1
        for idx, p in enumerate(profits):
            if p > best:
                best = p
                best_idx = idx
        return (best_idx + 1) if best > 0 else -1

solutions.python:
class Solution:
    def minOperationsMaxProfit(self, customers, boardingCost, runningCost):
        waiting = 0
        rotations = 0
        best_profit = -1
        best_rotation = -1
        profit = 0
        i = 0
        n = len(customers)
        while i < n or waiting > 0:
            if i < n:
                waiting += customers[i]
                i += 1
            board = min(4, waiting)
            waiting -= board
            rotations += 1
            profit += board * boardingCost - runningCost
            if profit > best_profit:
                best_profit = profit
                best_rotation = rotations
        return best_rotation if best_profit > 0 else -1
